ORDER DENYING DEFENDANT HOMEBRIDGE, INC.'S MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT HOMEBRIDGE, INC.'S MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION IS DENIED AS TO STACY CURTIS' SECOND, THIRD, AND FOURTH CAUSES OF ACTION FOR RETALIATION, HARASSMENT, AND FAILURE TO PREVENT HARASSMENT UNDER THE FAIR EMPLOYMENT AND HOUSING ACT (FEHA). THE COURT UNDERSTANDS THAT MS. CURTIS INTENDS TO FILE A REQUEST FOR DISMISSAL AS TO THE FITH CAUSE OF ACTION ONLY. ACCORDINGLY THE MOTION TO COMPEL IS DENIED IN ITS ENTRIETY. (AS FULLY REPORTED IN THE REPORTER'S NOTES/TRANSCRIPT) SEE WORD MINUTES FOR COMPLETE DETAILS. ORDER IS SIGNED. (SEE SCANNED ORDER) JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN, CLERK: W. 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT FILED BY PLAINTIFF CURTIS, STACEY INDIVIDUALLY AND ON BEHALF OF OTHER MEMBERS OF THE GENERAL PUBLIC SIMILARLY SITUATED AS TO DEFENDANT DOES 1 TO 20 HOMEBRIDGE HOME HEALTH CARE SERVICES, A CORPORATION BURNS, MARK AN INDIVIDUAL

LAW AND MOTION 302, HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF STACEY CURTIS' MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT. (SEE MINI-MINUTES FOR FULL DETAILS) THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF STACEY CURTIS' MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT IS GRANTED. "THERE IS A STRONG POLICY IN FAVOR OF LIBERAL ALLOWANCE OF AMENDMENTS" TO PLEADINGS. (MESLER V. BRAGG MGMT. CO. (1985) 39 CAL.3D 290, 296.) DEFENDANT HOMEBRIDGE, INC. HAS NOT DEMONSTRATED THAT IT WILL BE PREJUDICED BY THE COURT ALLOWING PLAINTIFF TO FILE A THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT, PARTICULARLY BECAUSE THE ADDITIONAL ALLEGATIONS STEM FROM THE SAME GENERAL SET OF FACTS AND TRIAL IS NOT UNTIL MARCH 2020. IF DEFENDANT WISHES TO CHALLENGE THE SUFFICIENCY OF THE NEW ALLEGATIONS, THE APPROPRIATE MECHANISM FOR DOING SO IS BY WAY OF A DEMURRER OR A MOTION TO STRIKE. (SEE CALIFORNIA CASUALTY GEN. INS. CO. V. SUPERIOR COURT (1985) 173 CAL.APP.3D 274, 281 ["THE PREFERABLE PRACTICE WOULD BE TO PERMIT THE AMENDMENT AND ALLOW THE PARTIES TO TEST ITS LEGAL SUFFICIENCY BY DEMURRER, MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS OR OTHER APPROPRIATE PROCEEDINGS."].) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN, CLERK: SEAN KANE, NOT REPORTED. (302/EPS)

calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Mar-13-2020 Vacated

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Jun-26-2019 Set For Trial On Mar-16-20 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION TO STRIKE 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-21-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances.

RULING: There being no appearance, or objection, the tentative ruling on DEFENDANT HOMEBRIDGE, INC.'s MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF THE 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT is adopted as follows:

Defendants' motion to strike the third amended complaint is granted with leave to amend. In her late-filed opposition to Defendants' demurrer and motion to strike and accompanying declaration, Plaintiff does not meaningfully respond to the motion to strike, but seeks leave to amend the third amended complaint, and asserts that she needs time to consult an attorney in order to do so. Plaintiff is granted 20 days leave, until December 11, 2019, to file and serve a further amended complaint; no further extensions will be granted. If Plaintiff fails to timely file a fourth amended complaint, Defendants may appear ex parte to seek an order of dismissal with prejudice and entry of judgment. (Code Civ. Proc., 581(f)(2); Cal. R. Ct. 3.1320(h).) Plaintiff is admonished that this is her last opportunity. (See Code Civ. Proc., 430.41(e)(1) ["In response to a demurrer and prior to the case being at issue, a complaint . . . shall not be amended more than three times, absent an offer to the trial court as to such additional facts to be pleaded that there is a reasonable possibility the defect can be cured to state a cause of action"].)

Counsel for the defendants shall prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling and submit it in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not reported.

DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-21-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances.

RULING: There being no appearance, or objection, the tentative ruling on DEFENDANT HOMEBRIDGE, INC.'s DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT is adopted as follows:

Defendants' demurrer to the third amended complaint is sustained in its entirety with leave to amend. In her late-filed opposition to Defendants' demurrer and motion to strike and accompanying declaration, Plaintiff does not meaningfully respond to the demurrer, but seeks leave to amend the third amended complaint, and asserts that she needs time to consult an attorney in order to do so. Plaintiff is granted 20 days leave, until December 11, 2019, to file and serve a further amended complaint; no further extensions will be granted. If Plaintiff fails to timely file a fourth amended complaint, Defendants may appear ex parte to seek an order of dismissal with prejudice and entry of judgment. (Code Civ. Proc., 581(f)(2); Cal. R. Ct. 3.1320(h).) Plaintiff is admonished that this is her last opportunity. (See Code Civ. Proc., 430.41(e)(1) ["In response to a demurrer and prior to the case being at issue, a complaint . . . shall not be amended more than three times, absent an offer to the trial court as to such additional facts to be pleaded that there is a reasonable possibility the defect can be cured to state a cause of action"].)

Counsel for the defendants shall prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling and submit it in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not reported.

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Leave To File Third Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Aug-26-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES: Plaintiff STACEY CURTIS (Tel: 415-678-0093) appearing in pro per;
No other appearances made in this matter.

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF STACEY CURTIS' Motion For Leave To File Third Amended Complaint.

Matter submitted without argument. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Plaintiff Stacey Curtis' motion for leave to file third amended complaint is granted. "There is a strong policy in favor of liberal allowance of amendments" to pleadings. (Mesler v. Bragg Mgmt. Co. (1985) 39 Cal.3d 290, 296.) Defendant Homebridge, Inc. has not demonstrated that it will be prejudiced by the Court allowing Plaintiff to file a third amended complaint, particularly because the additional allegations stem from the same general set of facts and trial is not until March 2020. If Defendant wishes to challenge the sufficiency of the new allegations, the appropriate mechanism for doing so is by way of a demurrer or a motion to strike. (See California Casualty Gen. Ins. Co. v. Superior Court (1985) 173 Cal.App.3d 274, 281 ["the preferable practice would be to permit the amendment and allow the parties to test its legal sufficiency by demurrer, motion for judgment on the pleadings or other appropriate proceedings."].)

Order signed in open court.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: Sean Kane, Not Reported. (302/EPS)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-28-2018 Continued To May-16-18 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration
Off Calendar Mar-26-2018 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Hannibal P. Odisho, Esq. of Allen Glaessner Hazelwood Werth, T:415.697.3463, for defendant/moving party;
Ashley Pellouchoud, Esq. of Ottinger Law Firm, T:309.299.0160, for plaintiff/opposing party.

[In the tentative ruling, Judge Kahn, disclosed that counsel for Homebridge, Steven Werth, is a friend of his and coached his son's baseball team approximately seven years ago, but Judge Kahn believes that he is fair and impartial in this case. There were no objections from either party.]

RULING: The matter was argued. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopts the tentative ruling on DEFENDANT HOMEBRIDGE, INC.'s Motion To Compel Arbitration.

Defendant Homebridge, Inc.'s motion to compel arbitration of all individual claims and to dismiss the class claims alleged in plaintiff Stacey Curtis' first amended complaint is denied in its entirety. Ms. Curtis' statutory claims based on alleged violations of the Labor Code and Business and Professions Code 17200 are not arbitrable under the Collective Bargaining Agreement's arbitration clause. "In a case involving alleged statutory violations, the presumption of arbitrability that typically applies to contractual disputes arising out of a CBA is not applicable." (Vasserman v. Henry Mayo Newhall Memorial Hospital (2017) 8 Cal.App.5th 236, 245). The CBA must "contain a clear and unmistakable waiver of the covered employees' rights to a judicial forum relating to the statutory claims alleged in the complaint." (Id.). In determining "whether a collective bargaining agreement makes compliance with the statute a contractual commitment subject to the arbitration clause, the relevant factors are the generality of the arbitration clause, explicit incorporation of statutory requirements, and the inclusion of specific contractual provisions." (Id at 246). The CBA in this case has language that is nearly identical to the language of the arbitration agreement in Vasserman, where the court held that the Labor Code claims alleged in that case were not arbitrable. As in Vasserman, the arbitration clause in the CBA in this case "cannot be reasonably read to include an explicitly stated, clear and unmistakable waiver of a judicial forum for employees' statutory claims." (Id. at 247). As in Vasserman, the provisions of the CBA in this case related to wages (section 20) and hours (section 17) do not mention any statutory requirements or California law. Homebridge's argument that the foregoing rules enunciated in Vasserman are inapplicable to this case because this case is governed by the Federal Arbitration Act lacks merit. The Vasserman rules fully apply to cases governed by the FAA. (Cortez v. Doty Bros. Equipment Co. (2017) 15 Cal. App. 5th 1, 11-12 and n. 4; Mendez v. Mid-Wilshire Health Care Center (2013) 220 Cal. App. 4th 534, 543; Vasquez v. Superior Court (2000) 80 Cal. App. 4th 430, 435).

Counsel for plaintiff is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the Court's ruling and complies with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Reporter: Laura Reding, CSR 711, email: lreding@sftc.org.
